hypothesis: If catalytic surface compositions are generated in a chemically informed latent space (via a periodic-table autoencoder) and sampled by a conditional diffusion model given adsorbate identity, target adsorption energy, and allowed elements, then the resulting two-shell compositions will achieve lower Fréchet distance, lower leakage, and higher cosine similarity than a chemically agnostic baseline across data scales because the chemically structured latent space and conditioning capture and enforce relevant physico-chemical correlations.
